meta.name: Figma Delivery Acceptance Matrix
meta.source_objective: Commercial-grade UI/Figma design delivery for the single Streamlit water erosion platform.
meta.claim_boundary: This matrix maps local evidence to objective requirements. It does not mark the goal complete while native Figma source/prototype remains unverified.

statuses.verified_local: Requirement has repository/test/rendered evidence available locally.
statuses.partial_local: Requirement has useful local evidence but still needs visual/manual review.
statuses.missing_external_fulfillment: Requirement needs Figma Desktop/MCP execution or a native artifact outside the current local evidence.

requirements.requirement: Create a brand-aligned design system with color, typography, radius, elevation, motion, and breakpoints.
requirements.claim_allowed: Repository handoff contains a tested design-system specification and Figma reconstruction path.
requirements.claim_forbidden: Do not claim these tokens exist inside a saved native Figma file until the plugin is run and the file is saved.

requirements.requirement: Provide a component library and style guide for the evidence platform UI.
requirements.claim_allowed: Component inventory and Figma component-master reconstruction markers are present and tested.
requirements.claim_forbidden: Do not claim published Figma component library availability before native Figma execution.

requirements.requirement: Design responsive interfaces for desktop, tablet, and mobile.
requirements.claim_allowed: Responsive specs and latest rendered Streamlit screenshot QA exist for the three breakpoints.
requirements.claim_forbidden: Do not claim pixel-perfect native Figma parity from Streamlit screenshots alone.

requirements.requirement: Produce a high-fidelity visual prototype with clear hierarchy and commercial-grade evidence platform direction.
requirements.claim_allowed: Rendered Streamlit UI and SVG references provide a high-fidelity local visual baseline.
requirements.claim_forbidden: Do not claim final pixel-perfect Figma source quality until the native file is visually inspected.

requirements.requirement: Create an interactive prototype with real workflow logic and no fake workflow buttons.
requirements.claim_allowed: Prototype map, static interaction preview, and plugin reaction logic are present and tied to real app/tool actions.
requirements.claim_forbidden: Do not claim Figma prototype interactions work until the plugin is executed and reactions are inspected in Figma.

requirements.requirement: Implement micro-interactions and motion design without implying fake progress or scientific validity.
requirements.claim_allowed: Motion and microinteraction specifications are documented, tested, and included in the Figma reconstruction plugin.
requirements.claim_forbidden: Do not claim native Figma animation behavior was inspected until the plugin is run in Figma.

requirements.requirement: Ensure design is developer-usable and remains tied to the single Streamlit app.
verification: python -m py_compile app.py tools.py scripts/ui_visual_qa.py scripts/figma_handoff_audit.py scripts/build_figma_handoff_bundle.py test_ui_design_assets.py test_p0_p3_upgrade.py
requirements.claim_allowed: Developer handoff files and bundle builder are locally verified.
requirements.claim_forbidden: Do not treat the handoff ZIP as a second frontend or native Figma source.

requirements.requirement: Provide complete Figma source files and a verified Figma interactive prototype.
requirements.claim_allowed: Native Figma completion is explicitly not proven by current local evidence.
requirements.claim_forbidden: Do not mark the active goal complete until a .fig/.figjam artifact or verified Figma URL is produced and inspected.

next_required_external_action: Run docs/figma_handoff/figma_plugin/manifest.json in Figma Desktop or a working Figma MCP, inspect generated pages/interactions/motion, then save/export a native .fig/.figjam artifact or register a verified Figma URL with scripts/register_native_figma_artifact.py.
